REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1758 Quint St., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Rubke (Counsel for Gwendolyn Haynie, Personal Representative); Paul Fazio (representative of H.M. Vreeland & Son).

The Court noted at the outset that Petitioner had failed to notice the hearing to H.M. Vreeland & Son who had filed a Request for Special Notice. At the invitation of Mr. Rubke, Mr. Fazio represented to the Court that Vreeland would waive notice of the hearing.

After commencement of the sale and having received a final overbid in the amount of $650,000.00, the Court confirmed the sale of the real property in that amount. Upon advice of counsel that a bond could not be secured for the sale proceeds, the Court ordered the net proceeds from the sale placed in a blocked account and set a status hearing for the filing of the receipt of the blocked account for April 24, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Order Confirming Sale was signed subsequent to the hearing but in open court during the later calendar call.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Deputy Teresa Fox; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnsen.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Terminate Trust
Off Calendar Dec-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Clinton Killian (Counsel for Cheryl Haynie, Petitioner); Mark Rubke (Counsel for Gwendolyn Haynie, Personal Representative); Cherly Haynie (Petitioner, present in Court).

Counsel addressed the predominate issue as to whom should serve as the successor trustee for the Trust. Mr. Killian argued that under the facts of this case, the Probate Code allows only the beneficiaries to nominate the successor whereas Mr. Rubke argued it was in the best interest of all concerned that an independent Trustee be appointed. The Court indicated that its inclination was to appoint an independent successor trustee but took the matter under submission for further consideration.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR# 7782; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnsen.
